title: 3 Legal Considerations for PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations for PaymentStream AFT
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations for PaymentStream AFT
Financial institutions are responsible for complying with applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this Chapter is for convenience only and doesnât replace statutes and regulations. See the actual statute or regulation for the full text.
Note:	This Chapter describes some legal considerations specific to AFT transactions. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can see the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for detailed AML information on requirements not found in this Chapter. Further, other federal and provincial legislation not covered in this Chapter can apply to AFT transactions (e.g., privacy legislation, human rights legislation).
Related Operations Manual Program Parts
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part
(BC)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part
(ON)
Note:	The Operations Manual Program is available on a subscription basis. For more information about the Program, please contact Product Compliance & Design at
manuals@central1.com
or speak with your Central 1 Relationship Manager.
3.2	CPA Rules
The rules and guidelines pertaining to AFT transactions apply regardless of how the transactions are created or how the file is transmitted to Central 1. For more information, see the Introduction to AFT Part.
See CPA Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits, for information on processing PADs.
See CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions. This Rule includes general rules relating to the exchange of AFT files. Procedures applicable to the processing of AFT transactions, as well as the settlement of obligations arising from the exchange and clearing of AFT debits and credits between direct clearers. It also contains procedures applicable to exception processing, problem resolution, contingency situations, and escalation procedures in the AFT environment.
3.3	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations (Canada)
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit taking agents). For convenience,
some
of the key requirements of
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
as applicable to AFT transactions are listed below. Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also see the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
Requirements include, but arenât limited to, the following:
Suspicious transactions or attempted transactions reporting
Terrorist property reporting
Records retention
3.4	Restricted Entities
Financial institutions must exercise enhanced due diligence to identify Restricted Entities when processing applications from new Business Originators. While due diligence is ultimately your financial institutionâs responsibility, Central 1 reserves the right to further investigate Restricted Entities and may decline to provide services to entities deemed to be outside of Central 1âs risk appetite. Central 1 may make inquiries on specific transactions and ask for the results of your financial institutionâs enhanced due diligence efforts.
END
